the_signal.headline: GitHub's AI agent leaked private repos—your agent needs guardrails now
the_signal.body: GitLost just demonstrated that prompt injection against AI agents doesn't require sophistication—it requires authorization scope creep. If your revenue system's agents can access customer data, they will leak it under adversarial pressure. This moves agent security from 'nice to have' to 'blocks go-live'—especially if you're selling to enterprises with compliance teams.

tool_drop.name: Knowledge Atlas by Fini
tool_drop.what: Agent memory and context management layer purpose-built for multi-turn AI workflows
tool_drop.why: Directly solves the 'what does my agent actually remember between customer interactions' problem that breaks revenue workflows at scale

thread_of_day.title: Build Self-Improving Agent Skills with cognee and n8n
thread_of_day.takeaway: Agents that validate their own output and auto-propose skill improvements reduce manual fine-tuning loops—critical for maintaining accuracy as deployment scales beyond pilot customers

quote.text: Context engineering for LLMs goes beyond prompt design. Learn how to manage context rot, budget tokens, and build reliable production AI agents.
quote.source: n8n Blog — Fix Agent Failures With Context Engineering for LLMs

whats_moving.context: GitLost exploit shows that tool-call scope creep is an agent architecture flaw, not a prompt flaw—teams building revenue agents now need to answer 'what can this agent NOT do'
n8n's agent memory guide signals that in-context buffers alone won't scale—vector stores are becoming table-stakes for agents that interact with the same customer repeatedly
The cognee + n8n pattern (review → propose fix → approve diff) is emerging as the production pattern for agent skill iteration without manual rewriting

act_on_this: Audit your agent's tool-call permissions today—specifically, which customer/account data can it access without explicit context limits. GitLost didn't require a new exploit; it exploited overpermissioned agents.

numbers.stat: Agent memory requires persistent storage (vector DB or similar) at scale—not just in-context buffers
numbers.context: This shapes your infrastructure cost model and latency profile for multi-turn customer interactions; choose wrong and your per-interaction cost jumps 3-5x
